message.content: Adversaries may exfiltrate data using physical media like USB drives, external hard drives, or other removable storage devices. This technique is particularly relevant in scenarios involving air-gapped networks, where traditional network-based exfiltration methods are not feasible. The physical medium can serve as the final exfiltration point or facilitate data transfer between isolated systems. The impact of this technique includes unauthorized data transfer, potential data breaches, and loss of sensitive information. Common adversary methods include physically accessing the target environment to connect the removable media or leveraging insiders to introduce the device. Mitigation strategies involve strict control and monitoring of physical access to sensitive areas, implementing policies to restrict the use of removable media, and employing data loss prevention (DLP) solutions to detect and block unauthorized data transfers. Regular audits and employee training on security policies can further reduce the risk of data exfiltration via physical media.
